Title: Xin Zhao
Age: At deathAround 43 years old Timeline BornAround 946 AN DeathAround 989 AN Edit
Current residence: Citadel of Dawn, Great City of Demacia

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent: Xin Zhao was born in Ionia, raised on a fisherman's ship named Viscero. While serving as a cabin boy, Xin Zhao met Bo Yang, whom he eventually became close with and saw as a brother. One day, they ended up crossing into the waters of Noxus, where a Noxian ship captured them and took them to Noxus. Xin Zhao managed to kill one his captors to protect the crew, impressing the Noxians. When they reached Noxus, they let Xin Zhao and Bo Yang go, while the others were taken to the Reckoning Pits. Unfamiliar with this new land, Xin Zhao rose above the ranks with his expert spearmanship, so Noxus offered him a chance to live a better life. He reluctantly accepted and entered the arenas of the Reckoners and took the name Viscero after his first home, and Bo Yang followed him.

children.title: The Great Viscero
children.textContent: Viscero was a famous warrior amongst the arena, with the crowd cheering for the carnage he inflicted while the other Reckoners grew to fear him. But eventually, Boram Darkwill made a decree to privately free Noxian mercenaries, prisoners or Reckoners—including Xin Zhao and Bo Yang—to send them to fight for Noxus in other nations. Xin Zhao was transported west to the coastal fortress of Kalstead, an allied nation of Demacia, where he faced against the king's elite soldiers in the Dauntless Vanguard. Most of his fellow Reckoners ran away, but Xin Zhao stood his ground, taking down many of the Demacians. However, Bo Yang was killed in battle. When he was confronted by soldiers, he welcomed them to execute him, but he was instead spared and imprisoned. At his request, Xin Zhao was taken to meet their king, Jarvan III.

children.title: Service to King Jarvan III
children.textContent:
While Xin Zhao was repeatedly threatened by Sword-Captain Tianna Crownguard, Jarvan III was curious to hear Xin Zhao's story and gave him a chance to speak. After their conversation, he offered Xin Zhao a bag of money to go back to his real home in Raikkon, but since Demacia had shown him the true meaning of the ideals he strove for, he wanted to join his guard, which came as a shock to Tianna. The king asked how Xin Zhao could prove he was capable, so he snatched a guard's spear and fought off the guards before pointing the spear at the king's throat. He apologized and prepared to be killed, but Jarvan III forgave him and accepted him into his ranks. Tianna was still distrustful of him, but Xin Zhao swore an oath to her that if something happened to Jarvan III, she would kill him by her own hand.

Years passed, and Xin Zhao grew to become very close friends with Jarvan III, being chosen as his seneschal and bodyguard. Xin Zhao also became a beloved mentor to the young Prince Jarvan, hoping to raise him into an honorable king. To this end, Xin Zhao trained Jarvan IV in martial techniques and the myriad arts of war, and shared his knowledge of the spiritual philosophies of his old homeland of Ionia. Few were as loyal to the crown as Xin Zhao, and he believed that Demacia was where he truly belonged, serving them not for duty, but his own choice.

As Seneschal, Xin Zhao had a high position and could have chosen any room in the Citadel of Dawn. He chose a small room next to the Palace Gardens, which reminded him of Ionia. Xin Zhao also became acquainted with the palace library, which he perused for knowledge and would share with the king in his spare time. He became like family to the king and the prince, and was honored as one of the kingdom's finest warriors.

children.title: Meeting with Master Kennen
children.textContent: More than thirty years after leaving the land of his birth, Xin Zhao found Spirit Blossoms blooming from a sapling that he tended to in the royal gardens of Demacia, which were believed to never bloom beyond the First Lands. On the advice of his liege-lord Jarvan, Xin Zhao sailed halfway across the world to the Navori province of Ionia, taking a royal vessel and guards with him in search of answers. At the Spirit Blossom Festival, Xin Zhao met Kennen of the Kinkou Order, who was initially displeased that the Seneschal had brought a weapon into the holy festival. Still, Kennen could see something was troubling Xin and filled him in on all that had happened in Ionia during his absence: the Noxian invasion, and the occupation, during which time not a single blossom bloomed. Kennen also discussed with Xin Zhao the Tale of the Devoted Shrine Maiden, who followed her patron spirit into exile after the War of the False Kanmei. Kennen then invited Xin Zhao to the Kinkou Temple of Koeshin, where he planted the sapling in Ionian soil at the top of the steps.

children.title: Battle of Koeshin
children.textContent:
As Xin Zhao and Kennen were meditating at the top of the mountain, they were interrupted by a magical surge that heralded an invasion by demonkin from the spirit realm. As Kennen left to speak with Masters Shen and Mayym, Xin went to fight the creatures on his own. During the battle, he was unexpectedly aided by another Kinkou warrior named Yunara, and he learned of a second, secret temple on the mountain known as the Temple of Twilight. The two of them fought back against the demons and their leader, a demonic pretender who took the form of the Grovemother from Ionian folklore. Their battles with the pretender led them to defend the villagers, as other wicked forces stalked the forests around them.

At the end of the conflict, Xin Zhao and Yunara had successfully slain the pretender, though the spear given to him by King Jarvan was broken in the process. They left to confer with Kennen, and though Yunara demanded to speak with Shen, Kennen simply reacted with shock upon seeing her, as her post had been abandoned. Shortly after, a bell rang from the Temple of Twilight, signalling the wards had been breached. As they left to investigate, Yunara warned Xin Zhao that their "true test" lay ahead of them.

While going to the Temple of Twilight, Xin Zhao started having dark visions of a corrupted Demacia in ruins. Disturbed by this, Xin Zhao demanded to know who they were up against, Yunara and Kennen shared the truth about Zaahen and the Great Darkin War. The breach at the temple had convinced them that the Darkin had returned and intended to free Zaahen, and though they urged Xin Zhao to flee and warn Demacia, he chose to stay and fight, as the Darkin posed a threat to the entire world. Arriving at the mountain where the Temple of Twilight had emerged, they discovered it had already returned to the Spirit Realm, and to get access to it they needed to face the Trials of Twilight. Xin Zhao donned new garb and attached his spearhead to a new shaft grown from the sapling he brought to Ionia, after which he and Yunara traveled with the help of Kennen to the Spirit Realm to begin their trials.

children.title: Trials of Twilight
children.textContent:
Upon entering the Spirit Realm, Xin Zhao and Yunara got split into their own individual trials. In his own, Xin Zhao went back to his old life on the Viscero, seeing visions of his friend Bo Yang, who recounted the day the young Xin Zhao killed a grown Noxian with a mop stick when their ship was captured. Although Xin Zhao and Bo Yang were cut loose when landing in the capital, his moment of defiance had sent their entire crew into the Reckoning pits where they would meet their deaths, while Xin Zhao himself would carry on to become a Reckoner champion. The vision then turned to Bo Yang's death at the Battle of Kalstead against the Demacians, where he questioned Xin Zhao's decision to fight as he died in his arms.

In the next vision, Xin Zhao saw his conversation with King Jarvan III right before he was sent to Ionia to investigate the Wyldbloom. Their dialogue was not as the Seneschal remembered however, as the king was acting cold, dismissive, and unkind in a way that was unlike him. While King Jarvan was speaking, a Darkin bearing the form of Garen cut him down with his sword, after which Xin Zhao immediately avenged his liege-lord by driving his three-talon spear into the Darkin's throat. Behind him, another Darkin bearing the form of Prince Jarvan IV arrived and taunted him by foretelling the doom of Demacia. As he was at the god-warrior's mercy, Xin Zhao saw no other option than to grab the Darkin sword in the ground Garen had left behind, believing it was the only way to win the battle ahead. However, a mysterious voice warned him that it wasn't the answer he was searching and both the sword and the illusion vanished.

When his part of the Trial ended, he regrouped with Yunara within the Temple of Twilight and learned that she too heard the same voices. After talking about it, the two agreed that there was still some good in Zaahen and that they would protect him from whoever was trying to break into his prison.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent:
Xin Zhao was a human man with fair skin, brown eyes, and a muscular build. He had long black hair with a white streak, usually tied back in a ponytail.

He was known for wearing Demacian armor in battle, silvery white with gold and blue highlights. However, it was fashioned in a uniquely asymmetrical style, seemingly showing the Ionian influence. For example, his blue Demacian tabard was folded over one shoulder. He also wore pastel purple cloth underneath the armor, which was visible on the unarmored parts of his body, including his thighs and elbows.

As a Reckoner, he wore Noxian gladiatorial armor. In his homeland of Ionia, he has been seen wearing simple, traditional robes. As a child aboard the Viscero, he also wore simple Ionian fishing gear, including tabi and a wide-brimmed straw hat.

His signature weapon was a two-handed tri-tip spear for use in melee combat.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent: Xin Zhao was a disciplined warrior and fiercely loyal. Having endured a life of hardship, he did not hesitate when called to battle. However, he much preferred the virtuous ideals of Demacia over the needless death and brutality of the Noxian fighting pits. Despite being a fearsome combatant, confident in his martial prowess, he often spoke gently and had an aura of calm. Still, he could show aggression and anger when confronted by his enemies or those who wished to do harm. Overall, he was a thoughtful and conscientious individual who fought primarily to protect the weak and the innocent.

children.title: Jarvan Lightshield III
children.textContent: Xin Zhao and Jarvan III first met after the Battle of Kalstead, where the famed Reckoner was given a chance to go in peace after sharing how he had been captured and forced to fight for in the Noxian arenas in order to survive. Xin Zhao's story had resonated with King Jarvan, who likened it to the tales of Orlon and his followers seeking refuge from the world's many evils. Xin Zhao, too, had been moved by Jarvan's show of mercy, and judging him an honorable man, asked to enter his service as one of his guard. The king granted Xin Zhao's request, and in time, the former Viscero became Jarvan III's honored seneschal and trusted advisor, and one of his best friends.

children.title: Jarvan Lightshield IV
children.textContent: Xin Zhao was the devoted protector of Jarvan IV and his father, Jarvan III, being chosen as the king's seneschal some time after the Battle of Kalstead. Xin Zhao had been a beloved mentor to the prince since he was a young boy, training him in history and politics, as well as the myriad arts of war and the spiritual philosophies of his distant homeland in Ionia. Xin Zhao was a trusted advisor and friend to both Jarvans, and the prince considered him like family.

children.title: Tianna Crownguard
children.textContent: Xin Zhao swore an oath to Tianna Crownguard that she may kill him should anything happen to King Jarvan III.

mainContent.textContent:
